reasoning: Based on prediction market data and bookmaker odds as of October 2025, Elon Musk is considered a notable contender for TIME's 2025 Person of the Year, but he is not the favorite. He trails behind contenders such as Donald Trump, AI / AI Leaders, and the newly elected Pope Leo XIV (who is listed as the 7/4 favorite in some bookmaker odds). Musk previously won the award in 2021; TIME rarely names the same individual twice in such a short span unless they are a U.S. President. While his role leading the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) in the Trump administration keeps him highly influential, his odds have reportedly slipped since earlier in the year. The implied probability of his betting odds (around +500 to +800 currently, down from +250 in January) places his chances at approximately 10-15%. Considering the strong competition from the new Pope, AI, and Trump, an 11% probability is well-calibrated.
